title: csrf
description: A forged HTTP request tricks an authenticated admin into unknowingly changing account settings on the corporate portal while visiting a malicious page.

timeline.title: Unexpected Account Changes
timeline.description: Admin notices their corporate portal account email and recovery phone have been changed to unknown values without their action.

timeline.title: CSRF Attack Identified
timeline.description: Security review reveals the portal lacks CSRF tokens. An admin visited a phishing page containing a forged POST request that was automatically submitted.

timeline.title: Multiple Admins Affected
timeline.description: Two additional admin accounts show similar unauthorised changes after visiting the same malicious URL shared via a spear-phishing email.

timeline.title: Accounts Recovered
timeline.description: All affected accounts restored. Admin sessions invalidated. Malicious email recalled and quarantined.

timeline.title: CSRF Tokens Implemented
timeline.description: Development team deploys synchroniser CSRF tokens on all state-changing endpoints. SameSite cookie attribute set to Strict.

timeline.title: Security Headers Validated
timeline.description: Full application review confirms CSRF tokens present on all forms. Penetration test validates CSRF protections. Incident closed.

node-1.title: Unauthorised Account Changes Detected
node-1.situation: An admin reports their corporate portal account email address and 2FA recovery phone were changed without their knowledge. Server logs show a POST request to /account/settings coming from the admin's browser session, but the admin was on a different website at the time. CSRF attack is suspected.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Restore the admin account to correct settings, invalidate all active admin sessions, and investigate which malicious page triggered the forged request.

options.label: B
options.text: Ask the admin to change their password and assume their browser was compromised by malware.

options.label: C
options.text: Restore account settings and close the ticket as a user error without further investigation.

node-2.title: Identify the Malicious Page and Scope
node-2.situation: Browser history shows the admin visited a URL from a phishing email just before the changes occurred. Two other admins received the same email. You need to check if they are also affected and prevent further exploitation.
node-2.question: How do you contain the incident?

options.label: A
options.text: Audit all admin accounts for unauthorised changes, recall and quarantine the phishing email from all mailboxes, and block the malicious URL at the web proxy.

options.label: B
options.text: Only fix the reported admin's account and send an advisory email without recalling the phishing message.

node-3.title: Remediate the CSRF Vulnerability
node-3.situation: The portal's state-changing endpoints lack CSRF token validation, making them vulnerable to forged cross-site requests. The development team needs to implement CSRF protections immediately.
node-3.question: How do you remediate the CSRF vulnerability?

options.label: A
options.text: Implement synchroniser CSRF tokens on all state-changing forms and API endpoints, set SameSite=Strict on session cookies, and validate the Origin/Referer header server-side.

options.label: B
options.text: Add a CAPTCHA to the account settings page as the sole CSRF mitigation measure.

node-4.title: Validate CSRF Controls Application-Wide
node-4.situation: CSRF tokens are deployed on the account settings page. However, other state-changing endpoints across the application may also be vulnerable.
node-4.question: How do you ensure complete coverage?

options.label: A
options.text: Conduct a full application review to identify all state-changing endpoints, verify CSRF tokens are present on each, and commission a penetration test to validate.

options.label: B
options.text: Only apply CSRF tokens to the account settings endpoint since that is the one that was exploited.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The CSRF attack was contained, all affected admin accounts were restored, and the malicious email was recalled. CSRF tokens and SameSite cookie attributes now protect all state-changing endpoints, verified by a successful penetration test.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
